forecast-period.text: A trough will contract off the east coast of Victoria late Wednesday evening with a cold front set to follow on Thursday. A cold southerly airstream will establish in the wake of this front with another trough lingering in Bass Strait into Thursday evening. This trough will linger off the Gippsland coast on Friday with a low pressure system establishing in the Tasman Sea. A high pressure system will begin to slowly move in from the west on Friday, centring itself over Victoria on Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly in the evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres by early evening.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots increasing to 20 to 25 knots in the morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southerly 25 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 25 knots before dawn.
2 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Southeast to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Cloudy.
